data.title: Bitcoin Sent to Satoshi Address for Second Straight Day, Arkham Says
data.description: Arkham said someone has continued sending Bitcoin to the Satoshi address. According to Foresight News, data shows that over the past two days, one or more Revolut users have sent more than $5,000 worth of Bitcoin to the address. The motive remains unclear.

data.title: HMRC Recovers Over 8 Million Pounds From Crypto Tax Settlements
data.description:
The U.K. tax authority has recovered more than 8 million pounds after three years of cracking down on crypto tax evasion, following settlements with hundreds of cryptocurrency investors. According to Foresight News, data obtained through freedom of information requests showed that 502 crypto investors reached disclosure settlement agreements with the U.K. tax authority over unpaid taxes in the past two years.

In the 2024/25 tax year, 280 people settled with HMRC and paid a total of 3.5 million pounds. In the 2025/26 tax year, the number of settlements fell to 222, but the total amount rose to about 4.8 million pounds.

The U.K. Financial Conduct Authority said in analysis published this year that about 8% of U.K. adults, or around 4.5 million people, own crypto assets, more than double the level seen since 2020. The analysis estimated the average value of U.K. crypto holdings at 2,250 pounds, while most holders have less than 1,000 pounds invested and about 16% hold more than 5,000 pounds.

data.title: U.S. 8-Week Treasury Auction Bid-to-Cover Ratio Falls to 2.31
data.description: According to Jin10, the U.S. 8-week Treasury auction bid-to-cover ratio was 2.31, compared with a previous reading of 2.84.

data.title: IMAX gains on 'The Odyssey' box office strength and 2026 growth outlook
data.description:
According to CNBC, IMAX shares rose more than 8% on Thursday after the company said it remained on track to deliver a record $1.4 billion in global box office this year, helped by Universal's "The Odyssey." The film generated $52 million in global box office for IMAX over its opening weekend, with IMAX locations accounting for less than 1% of total screens but 20% of the movie's worldwide debut. IMAX CEO Rich Gelfond said on Thursday's earnings call that the film added another $11 million on Monday and $10.6 million on Tuesday, which he called the company's best Tuesday performance ever.

The company also said it installed 38 IMAX systems globally in the second quarter, up from 36 a year earlier, and has a backlog of 421 contracts to build screens. Chief Financial Officer Natasha Fernandes said the second-quarter total was the highest in a decade and that IMAX is on pace for 160 to 175 installations by the end of 2026.

data.title: Several Israeli Cities Open Public Shelters Amid Higher Missile Risk
data.description: Several Israeli cities, including Ramat Gan, Herzliya, Eilat and Karmiel, opened public shelters on July 23 as tensions between the United States and Iran escalated, raising fears of possible Iranian missile attacks, according to Jiemian News. Ramat Gan city authorities said their security assessment showed the risk of an Iranian missile strike on Israel had risen over the weekend. Eilat’s municipal government said staff were inspecting the shelters that had been opened and urged residents to follow safety instructions.

data.title: Sinners Win Probability Drops to 29.5% on Polymarket
data.description: The win probability for Sinners in Polymarket’s submarket for the Counter-Strike match between Sinners and FUT Esports fell sharply to 29.5% from 44.5% one hour earlier, a 15-point drop. According to ChainCatcher, Catcher Predict monitoring noted the sudden move and said related breaking news may be affecting the market.

data.title: UK Grid to Limit Power Exports to Europe More Often After Heat Wave
data.description:
The UK’s grid operator said it will restrict capacity on its power cables to Europe more frequently after the country faced an unusually tight balance between electricity supply and demand during a heat wave, according to Bloomberg.

The tighter limits are set to take effect as the operator responds to pressure on the grid.

data.title: Microsoft 365 Status: Service Issues Under Investigation
data.description: Microsoft 365 said it is investigating reports of service issues.

data.title: Gulf States and Jordan: Iranian Attacks on Our Territory, Citizens, and Civilian Facilities Continue
data.description: According to Jin10, the Gulf states and Jordan said Iranian attacks on their territory, citizens, and civilian facilities continue.

data.title: SEC Schedules September 17, 2026 Roundtable on 24-Hour U.S. Stock Trading
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission said it will hold a roundtable on September 17, 2026, to discuss the transition of the U.S. stock market to 24-hour trading. According to Foresight News, the meeting will cover preparations for overnight trading, the operation and resilience of a 24-hour market, and the opportunities and challenges of expansion.

data.title: Whale Jasonleo Closes 2,741.712 BTC Short Position and Opens Leveraged Long
data.description: Whale trader Jasonleo closed a 2,741.712 BTC short position about 1 hour ago and booked a profit of $2.263 million. According to ChainCatcher, the same trader then opened a 4x leveraged long position of 2,015.916 BTC at an average entry price of $64,983.34.

data.title: Gulf States and Jordan: Iran's Attack Heightens Threats to Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan
data.description: According to Jin10, Gulf states and Jordan said in a joint statement that Iran's attack has especially increased threats to Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan.

data.title: Google Cloud customers spend about 50% more than commitments, Kurian says
data.description: According to CNBC, Google Cloud chief Thomas Kurian said existing customers are spending roughly 50% more than they commit to when they sign up for the company's products, helping drive second-quarter cloud growth. He said the higher spending reflects the strength of Google Cloud's product lineup and go-to-market execution, and noted that the trend is showing up in both top-line and operating income growth.

data.title: Oil Tops $100 as Houthi Red Sea Attacks Open New Front in Middle East Conflict
data.description:
Bloomberg reported that oil topped $100 a barrel for the first time in two months after Iran-backed Houthi militants said they attacked two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea, escalating the Middle East conflict and threatening deeper supply disruptions.

The Yemen-based Houthis said they fired missiles and drones at the vessels to enforce a blockade of Saudi ports announced this week. The attacks open a new front in the conflict, with the Iran war already snarling traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

Targeting the Red Sea imperils the critical detour route that the Saudis used to keep oil barrels flowing amid the Hormuz disruptions. Exports from major producer Kazakhstan are also endangered by the Russia-Ukraine war, and global oil inventories are depleted by months of conflict.

Brent futures have surged more than 30% since the start of the month to trade near the highest since late May. Iran has struck vessels in Hormuz while the US has bombarded the Islamic Republic over the past 12 days and restored a blockade of its ports.

Rapidan Energy Group president Bob McNally, a former White House official, said round 2 of the military conflict is going to be broader than round 1, with risks to both shipping and energy infrastructure.

U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to hit Iranian bridges and power plants and reiterated threats to strike a suspected nuclear site. Washington and Tehran have both played down the prospect of peace talks.

Some vessels are already avoiding the Red Sea route, with Indian state firms and other Asian buyers contemplating a switch to longer, costlier routes via the Suez Canal. Kazakhstan is being forced to stop piping crude to its main export terminal on Russia's Black Sea coast following drone attacks on tankers.

data.title: Google Discloses $811 Billion in Future Contract Spending Commitments
data.description:
Google said in a quarterly filing that its future contract spending commitments reached $811 billion by the end of June, up nearly $500 billion from three months earlier. According to Odaily, the amount is disclosed separately from Google’s capital expenditure budget and reflects purchases committed under supply agreements and outstanding purchase orders.

About $200.7 billion of the commitments are short-term purchases. The commitments cover chips, data centers, electricity, inventory, and content licenses. Alphabet said on Wednesday that it expects to spend about $195 billion to $205 billion in capital expenditures this year.

data.title: Abraxas Capital Transfers $223.53 Million to Exchanges in 7 Hours
data.description: Onchain Lens monitoring shows Abraxas Capital transferred approximately $223.53 million in assets to multiple cryptocurrency exchanges over the past 7 hours. According to NS3.AI, the firm also withdrew approximately $26.42 million in assets from exchanges during the same period.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia Says Black Sea Waters Are Unsafe for Ships
data.description: Russia warned that its Black Sea waters are unsafe for navigation after Ukraine stepped up attacks on ships, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | Cleveland-Cliffs Promotes CFO Celso Goncalves to President
data.description: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. promoted Chief Financial Officer Celso Goncalves to president, elevating the son of the steelmaker’s chief executive officer, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Summit Updates Ivonescimab OS Data and US FDA Review Timing
data.description:
Summit said the overall survival hazard ratio for ivonescimab plus chemotherapy in the HARMONi study fell to 0.76 in the all-treated population and in the Western subgroup, based on data through June 2026. The company said it has submitted the result to the US FDA. Jiemian News reported that the update extends the follow-up period and brings the Western subgroup result in line with the benefit seen in China’s phase 3 study.

HARMONi is a global phase 3 trial of ivonescimab plus chemotherapy as second-line treatment for EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer. In China, Akeso’s HARMONi-A phase 3 trial previously reported statistically positive results on both progression-free survival and overall survival, with PFS HR at 0.46 and OS HR at 0.74. Ivonescimab was first approved in mainland China in May 2024. Summit had previously reported OS HR of 0.79 for the all-treated population and 0.98 for the Western subgroup in an earlier readout, before the latest update lowered both figures to 0.76.

Summit filed the US marketing application for this indication in the fourth quarter of 2025, and the FDA target action date is November 14, 2026. Akeso said 2025 revenue was 3.056 billion yuan, including about 3.033 billion yuan in commercial sales, up 51% year on year, driven mainly by cadonilimab and ivonescimab. The company said ivonescimab has also been approved in mainland China for first-line PD-L1-positive non-small cell lung cancer, while its application for first-line advanced squamous non-small cell lung cancer has been accepted.

data.title: Russia’s Oil and Gas Tax Revenue Expected to Rise 60% in July
data.description: According to Reuters calculations, Russia’s state oil and gas tax revenue is expected to rise 60% year over year in July to 1.3 trillion rubles, benefiting from higher global oil prices.
